summary: Large item decompilation PR that replaced most placeholder stubs in src/melee/it/items/itgshell.c with typed C for the green shell item logic. It added the green shell item state table, local attribute struct, typed prototypes, and item-var layout details, producing 34 new full matches and four near-matches according to the decomp.dev bot. The author noted remaining inline-related issues, which is consistent with several functions using pragma dont_inline, PAD_STACK, and comments marking expected inlines.
what_changed: Implemented most of itgshell.c, growing it from placeholder markers to roughly 668 lines of C for green shell behavior.
Added a local itGShell_Attrs struct with a STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(itGShell_Attrs) == 64) and used article specialAttributes for shell physics/timing constants.
Added ItemStateTable_GShell with ten motion entries linking itGshell_UnkMotion*_Anim, _Phys, and _Coll callbacks.

Implemented shell lifecycle callbacks including Spawned, PickedUp, Thrown, Dropped, EnteredAir, DmgDealt, DmgReceived, Reflected, Clanked, HitShield, ShieldBounced, EvtUnk-related surrounding logic, and jumped-on callback it_8028CF68.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations in itgshell.h with typed Item_GObj* prototypes for many functions.

Expanded itGShell_ItemVars in itCommonItems.h from mostly padding into named fields xDD4, xDD8, xDDC, xDE0, xDE4, xDE8, bitfields xDEC_b0 through xDEC_b7, xE10, and Vec3 vel at ip+E20, with STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(itGShell_ItemVars) == 88).
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt for itgshell.c .sdata end from 0x804D5280 to 0x804D527D.
Bot report recorded matched code improvement of +4508 bytes and 34 new full matches in main/melee/it/items/itgshell, with four additional near-matches.
smart_moves: Used STATIC_ASSERTs on both the item variable struct and the green shell attributes struct to lock down layout while replacing padding with real fields.
Kept uncertain semantic names offset-based, such as xDD4 and xDEC_b1, rather than over-naming unknown state until behavior is better understood.
Matched repeated item callback patterns by factoring shared helper-like routines such as it_8028B988, it_8028B8D8, it_8028C3A8, and it_8028C898 and then invoking them from state callbacks.

Used item subsystem helpers already present in the codebase, including GET_ITEM, GET_JOBJ, Item_80268E5C, it_80272860, it_8026E414, it_8026D62C, itColl_BounceOffVictim, and itColl_BounceOffShield, instead of open-coding engine behavior.

Applied matching aids only where needed: PAD_STACK for stack shape, #pragma dont_inline around sensitive functions, direct gobj->user_data in one animation callback, and explicit empty returns in no-op phys callbacks.
Tracked the binary split side effect by updating the .sdata range in splits.txt.
decomp_lessons: For item files with large placeholder blocks, first recover the per-item item-var layout and specialAttributes layout; many subsequent motion callbacks become straightforward field reads and writes.
Offset-based names are acceptable for unknown item fields when paired with comments/static assertions; this preserves correctness without inventing semantics.
Item state tables are a central anchor for matching item behavior: once the motion IDs and callback signatures are typed, spawn/pickup/throw/collision functions can be connected systematically.

Inline behavior can dominate the last few bytes of matching. This PR's body explicitly says 'inlines causing a few issues still', and the diff uses dont_inline pragmas and inline comments to control or document those cases.
Near-matches can still be worth merging when they unlock many complete functions; bot data showed 34 full matches and four functions left at 98-99.95%.
When replacing padding in shared headers like itCommonItems.h, verify the total size so other item variants sharing the union/struct area are not disturbed.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions retained address-style names such as it_8028B8D8, it_8028B988, it_8028C3A8, and it_8028CF68.
Green shell motion callbacks use the existing pattern itGshell_UnkMotionN_Anim, itGshell_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itGshell_UnkMotionN_Coll.
Item lifecycle callbacks use it_3F14_Logic14_* names, e.g. Spawned, PickedUp, Thrown, Dropped, EnteredAir, DmgReceived, Reflected, Clanked, HitShield, and ShieldBounced.
Unknown item-var fields are named by item pointer offset, e.g. xDD4, xDD8, xDDC, xDE0, xDE4, xDE8, xE10.
Bitfields at ip+DEC are named xDEC_b0 through xDEC_b7.
Padding names encode offsets/ranges, e.g. pad_DF0 and pad_E14.
The item state table name added in C is ItemStateTable_GShell; the header still declares extern ItemStateTable it_803F5BA8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(4) and PAD_STACK(8) in functions where stack layout needed adjustment.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around itGshell_UnkMotion4_Anim and itGshell_UnkMotion8_Anim to address inline-sensitive matching.
Commented calls to it_8028B988 and it_8028B8D8 as inlined inside itGshell_UnkMotion6_Anim, matching the author's note that inlines were still problematic.
Used direct Item* access via gobj->user_data in itGshell_UnkMotion8_Anim instead of GET_ITEM(gobj), presumably for codegen shape.
Preserved exact no-op physical callbacks with explicit return statements.
Used ABS, HSD_Randf, HSD_Randi, HSD_JObjGetChild, and HSD_JObjAddRotationY in patterns matching expected shell velocity, randomness, and visual rotation behavior.
Adjusted the split .sdata end address to match the generated data layout after decompilation.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Author note: 'inlines causing a few issues still.'
decomp.dev bot reported 43.42% matched code overall for GALE01, +0.12% and +4508 bytes, with 34 new itgshell matches.
Bot identified four remaining near-matches: itGshell_UnkMotion6_Anim at 99.95%, it_8028BC2C at 99.90%, it_3F14_Logic14_HitShield at 98.24%, and it_3F14_Logic14_Clanked at 99.82%.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching itgshell.c to see whether the four near-matches from PR #1922 were completed.
Search for uses of itGShell_ItemVars fields to determine whether xDD4/xDD8/xDDC/xDE0/xDE4/xDE8 and xDEC bits later received semantic names.
Compare green shell itgshell.c against red shell itrshell.c for reusable item-state and collision patterns.
Check whether it_8028CF68 header return type was later normalized, since the diff excerpt shows the C implementation returning bool while the header declaration changed to int.
